data.content:
## Implementation Plan

### Problem
`nextKnot()` in `knots.ts:395-405` calls `execWrite` once and returns failure on any error, including transient "database is locked" errors.

### Solution
Add retry-with-exponential-backoff logic to `nextKnot()`. Retry up to 3 times with delays of 1s, 2s, 4s. Only retry on transient errors (LOCKED, TIMEOUT, UNAVAILABLE, RATE_LIMITED) using the existing `classifyErrorMessage()` + `isRetryableByDefault()` from `backend-errors.ts`.

### File Changes
1. `src/lib/knots.ts` - Add `sleep()` helper, add `retryOnTransient()` wrapper, update `nextKnot()` to use it
2. `src/lib/__tests__/knots-retry.test.ts` - New test file covering retry scenarios

### Test Strategy
- Test: retries on "database is locked" error up to 3 times
- Test: succeeds after transient failure (retry 1 fails, retry 2 succeeds)
- Test: does NOT retry on non-transient errors (e.g., "not found")
- Test: returns final error after exhausting retries

### Risks
- Minimal: retry logic is self-contained and only wraps the existing exec call
- Sleep delays are real wall-clock time in prod but will be mocked in tests
